pages.text: The $(bold)Simulation Wrench$() is your tool for $(bold)configuring PreFab faces$(), not for switching states.$(br2)$(bold)Shift + Right-click$() on a PreFab to open the face configuration GUI.$(br2)While held, the wrench also visualizes which PreFab faces are inputs and which are outputs.$(br2)$(bold)Note:$() To switch between states (BUILDING/SIMULATING/CACHED), use an $(italic)empty hand or non-wrench item$() to open the control menu.

pages.title: Configuration GUI
pages.text: $(bold)Shift + Right-click$() opens the face configuration screen where you can:$(br2)$(li)Select which face to configure$(li)Set mode (PULL/PUSH/DISABLED)$(li)Set resource filter (ITEMS/FLUIDS/ENERGY)$(li)Choose target Importer/Exporter

pages.title: Visual Indicators
pages.text: While holding the Simulation Wrench, PreFab faces show visual overlays:$(br2)$(li)Input faces (PULL mode)$(li)Output faces (PUSH mode)$(li)Disabled faces$(br2)This makes it easy to see your configuration at a glance.
